PROOF OF SERVICE TO THE PROPOSED JUDGMENT BASED ON THE ORDER AFTER COURT TRIAL (TRANSACTION ID # 64817578) FILED BY PETITIONER CHAN, ANNA CHAN, STEPHEN YETTING, NANCY CHAN, HELEN

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: TITLE TO 1323-1325 15TH AVE. IN SAN FRANCISCO, CA SHALL BE RESTORED TO ITS JULY 2009 STATUS WITHIN 14 CALENDAR DAYS OF THE JUDGMENT IN THIS MATTER; SPECIFICALLY, THAT 50.17391% OF THE AFORE-REFERENCED PROPERTY BE TRANSMITTED FROM THE CURRENT TITLE HOLDERS TO STEPHEN CHAN, AS SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE OF THE CHAN FAMILY B (OR EXEMPTION) TRUST. PETER CHAN SHALL BE REMOVED AS TRUSTEE OF THE CHAN FAMILY B TRUST, AND REPLACED AS TRUSTEE BY STEPHEN CHAN. PETITIONERS' REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES IS DENIED. RESPONDENTS' REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES IS DENIED.(SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT)

calendar.matters:
COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Feb-27-2020 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Continued To Feb-19-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 608

Petition For Orders
Off Calendar Nov-25-2019 Continued To Feb-18-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daniel Spector appeared by telephone for Petitioner.
Gerard Lam for Respondent/Objector.

Mr. Lam stated that mediation was unsuccessful, that he is here to have an evidentiary hearing set, that discovery is all done and that the issue is whether the trust was funded with community assets or separate assets. Mr. Spector stated that asset of the B-trust is one half interest in a partial property, that Mr. Lam's Client removed it from the B-trust to his own trust and then removed it and transferred it to himself. Discussion was held regarding the number of witnesses, the length of the trial and whether or not it could be heard in probate. The Petition for Orders is set for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on February 18, 2020 at 9:30 a.m., (order for the same signed in open court). A copy of the order and notice of trial was also provided to Mr. Lam in open Court. The Court Clerk will e-mail a copy of the order to Mr. Spector.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Orders
Off Calendar Sep-16-2019 Continued To Nov-25-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gerard Lam for Peter Chan (Respondent) and Qing Tam (Objector).
Daniel Spector appeared by telephone Chan Beneficiary Children.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than November 8, 2019. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by November 18, 2019. The Petition for Orders is continued to and the Parties are to return on November 25, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Parties are to file a status report as to the outcome of mediation and are to indicate what an evidentiary hearing will look like (length of time, witnesses, exhibits, etc.). A joint status report is preferable. The penciled in trial dates of November 8, 2019 and November 15, 2019 are removed from the Court's outlook calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Orders
Off Calendar Jul-01-2019 Continued To Sep-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daniel Spector appeared by telephone for Anna Chan, Stephen Chan, Helen Chan and Nancy Yetting (Petitioners).
Gerard Lam for Peter Chan (Respondent) and Qing Tam (Objector).

Mr. Spector stated that the matter will not require much discovery, that he is in the process of sending a deposition subpoena to the estate planner and that he would like the matter to be set for trial. Both parties indicated that they are amenable to mediation. The Petition for Orders is continued to September 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a status conference. The trial dates are penciled in for November 8, 2019 (half day) and November 15, 2019 (half day) at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Orders
Off Calendar May-20-2019 Continued To Jul-01-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Daniel Spector appeared by telephone for Anna Chan.
Gerard Lam for Peter Chan (Trustee).

Mr. Spector stated that the trust documents were delivered to him pursuant to the Court order, that he has received everything, that he will file copies of the documents, that all of the Beneficiaries received notice and that this involves funding of the by-pass trust for which there are five Beneficiaries (his 4 Clients and Mr. Lam's Client). Mr. Lam stated that there are additional Beneficiaries and that Peter's Spouse has an interest in the trust. Mr. Spector stated that Peter's Spouse is not a Beneficiary of the by-pass trust, that he would like the matter to be set for a short cause trial, that a trust can be modified, that he is happy to provide notice to the Spouse and that some depositions may be needed. The Court informed Counsel that it will not set a trial until discovery is completed. The Petition for Orders is continued to July 1, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Orders
Off Calendar May-15-2019 Continued To May-20-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Orders
Off Calendar May-06-2019 Continued To May-15-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Orders
Off Calendar Apr-08-2019 Continued To May-06-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Orders
Off Calendar Feb-20-2019 Continued To Apr-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Production Of Trust Agreement
Off Calendar Jan-03-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Daniel Spector, by CourtCall, for Petitioner Anna Chan; Gerard Lam for Qing Tam, wife of the trustor.

Mr. Lam stated that he had no objection to granting the petition, but was concerned that his client had not been given notice. Mr. Spector replied that he was seeking production of the bypass trust, to which Qing Tam is not a beneficiary. The Court granted the petition, subject to review of the order.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Anasse. (Not reported)

Petition To Compel Production Of Trust Agreement
Off Calendar Dec-17-2018 Continued To Dec-31-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
